title: Breadth-First Search, Queues, and Unweighted Shortest Paths
description: The queue uses slow front deletion, predecessors change after discovery, distances mix cells and pixels, and shortest is claimed on weighted edges. Learners cumulatively produce deque-based shortest-path solver through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Maintain a FIFO frontier whose discovered distances advance in nondecreasing edge count.
Use deque append and popleft with explicit membership state and bounded duplicate work.
Assign distance and predecessor at first discovery and reconstruct a valid route after reaching the goal.
